# Pixel_AI brainstorming

## The idea of Pixel-based

- Each spot in a tissue could be considered as mixture of tissue
 > archetypes (for example tumor infiltration contains xx% of tumor
 > and xx% of immune cells). But we don't know the archetypes yet.

- Finding the archetypes means finding the niches in tissue - this
 > will be the new framework of describing and analyzing a tissue in
 > spatial molecular profiling.

- The idea is to infer the archetype/features from multi-channel
 > informations.

 - Consider PCA but in an explainable way. Reduce the 30+
 > dimensions (#channel) into \<10 (# tissue) dimensions.

- We could use deep learning models to solve our feature extraction
 > task. For example currently we are using VAE model. But is it the
 > best?

## How to evaluate the performance of our model

- Visualize our latent space

 - UMAP, current no colors

 - Color by labels. But what label do we have?

- Define downstream tasks

 - We have molecular profile labels for cores, maybe we could use
 > latent space to predict molecular profile.

 - 

## How to overcome multi-channel problem

Most CV models are designed to process RGB images (3 dimension), but we
have 20+ channels (20+ dimension)

- Fusion.

## How to investigate more on spatial information

- Grad-CAM:

# Pixel_AI brainstorming

## The idea of Pixel-based

- Each spot in a tissue could be considered as mixture of tissue
 > archetypes (for example tumor infiltration contains xx% of tumor
 > and xx% of immune cells). But we don't know the archetypes yet.

- Finding the archetypes means finding the niches in tissue - this
 > will be the new framework of describing and analyzing a tissue in
 > spatial molecular profiling.

- The idea is to infer the archetype/features from multi-channel
 > informations.

 - Consider PCA but in an explainable way. Reduce the 30+
 > dimensions (#channel) into \<10 (# tissue) dimensions.

- We could use deep learning models to solve our feature extraction
 > task. For example currently we are using VAE model. But is it the
 > best?

## How to evaluate the performance of our model

- Visualize our latent space

 - UMAP, current no colors

 - Color by labels. But what label do we have?

- Define downstream tasks

 - We have molecular profile labels for cores, maybe we could use
 > latent space to predict molecular profile.

 - 

## How to overcome multi-channel problem

Most CV models are designed to process RGB images (3 dimension), but we
have 20+ channels (20+ dimension)

- Fusion.

## How to investigate more on spatial information

- Grad-CAM:

ost CV models are designed to process RGB images (3 dimension), but we
have 20+ channels (20+ dimension)

- Fusion.

## How to investigate more on spatial information

- Grad-CAM:
 > [[https://github.com/jacobgil/pytorch-grad-cam]{.underline}](https://github.com/jacobgil/pytorch-grad-cam)

- ViT frames (auto patch segmentation)

# 2024.01.25

- A baseline for the label prediction: input patches into a classifier
 > (raw_image_classifier) (NN, ResNet for 3 channels)

- And then compare with latent space

- The future goal is to train with full images (?)

### Datasets

### Model Architectures

es and embedded images

### Tasks

- Pretext tasks

### Downstream analysis + visualization

### Task assignment

The deep learning model will become the core, and we could define
different downstream tasks for everyone's thesis/future publications...

This September we should have a model that works well and apply it on
SAPCE.

#### Idea of tasks for Olavi: 

- What about his thesis? The higher goal is to get a good
 > representation of latent space...

- 0\. Refactor the code, and encapsulate frequently used scripts...
 > Ziqi will help

- 1\. Help with comparing raw image methods and embedding methods

 - Work on the ResNet branch Ziqi created

 - Label: pre and post-chemotherapy

 - Dataset: Patches and full cores

 - Experiment: (1) ResNet50+classifier on raw images of full
 > core (2) ResNet50+classifier on raw images of patches

 - These will be compared with Matias's Vanilla VAE experiments

- After step 1 we could conclude if **embedding performs better than
 > the raw image. And If full cores work better than patches**

- 2\. (All of us, under discussion)

 - Fine-tuning with current architecture;

 - choosing different channels... or changing the dimension of
 > Latent Space.

 - Change other architectures.

# Meeting note 2024.03.26

Timeline for automatic ROI selection: initially August, but flexible

Experiment SPACE:

- 92 samples (all PDS), whole slide images

- 4 cycles (20-plex)

- With H&E samples mapped with tCyCIF images

- Need automatic ROI selection for GeoMx

For whole slide images the computational resource is a restriction...
But keep in mind that we are not aiming for a solution to be able to
profile all kinds of data/tissue.

2 future branches:

- After get a good model (VAE-based?)

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Matias: output of TMEncoder + classifier + GradCAM = automatic ROI
 > selection

- Ziqi and Olavi: output of TMEncoder + feature mapping / tissue
 > cellular neighborhoods = unsupervised image profiling.

Tasks:

- Feature extraction from images (Olavi, Ziqi)

- Classifier + GradCAM: ROI selection (Matias)

- Identify spatial motifs / patterns to enable ROI selection (?)

Matias: write thesis in 2024 summer, graduate in 2025.

Olavi: thesis June, July, graduate in 2024.

- Collaboration with Lassi & Esa: low-plex staining, meet in April.

P.S. Create a Ganett chart to arrange tasks

her architectures.
